attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches from 150-250m out, often from disadvantaged positions (8th wheel or deeper). Excels at wheel-surfing and improvising through gaps rather than following linear leadout trains.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on dedicated leadout infrastructure (Quick-Step era). When leadout fails or is absent, shows adaptability by jumping wheel-to-wheel through final 500m. Performance collapses entirely without proper team support structure.
terrain_preference: Flat, pure sprint stages only. Completely exposed on any undulating or moderately hilly terrain where he gets distanced with multiple teammates, unable to contest intermediate sprints or defend positions.
vulnerability: Instantly non-competitive on any terrain with gradient. Struggles dramatically when switching teams without dedicated leadout (Quick-Step to other setup). Wind exposure causes significant fading. Form highly dependent on team infrastructure.
key_patterns: Wins from deep positions (8th+) via late slingshots, not pure leadout power (5 observations)
Dropped on moderate climbs with teammates, loses 12+ minutes easily (3 observations)
Peak adaptability when improvising through gaps after leadout failures (4 observations)
Performance collapses without dedicated Quick-Step infrastructure, invisible after team switch (3 observations)
Exceptional bike throw and timing in photo finishes, winning from half-length deficits (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2017 peak form (5 TdF wins) deteriorated sharply by mid-2018 after team switch, dropping to non-top-30 finishes without Quick-Step leadout infrastructure
